tax: Placed at family rank, Open-Flame Pyrics is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: an open visible-flame body. A new genus belongs here only if it shows an open-flame body with its own fuel mode.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a leaping flame-ball with heat-shimmer. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — scorch-rings and soot — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Open-Flame Pyrics occupies gas-seeps and oil stores. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where a spark ignites a fuel pocket and recur with the vent refills, sustaining themselves on open combustion of fuel.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: darts along fuel, igniting what it touches. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: open-flame body and self-light. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each genus and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: ignition and flare. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Open-Flame Pyrics matters because spreads fire through fuel stores. It lets survey reports sort open-flame hazards from smouldering ones without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Open-Flame Pyrics is the family-rank grouping of bright open-combustion fire elementals within the True Elementals. Its members share a leaping visible flame body that ignites on contact, and divide below into genus groups by flame size and fuel type. The brightest and fastest-spreading of the fire kin.
